question_text: Let $( Y , X _ { 1 } , X _ { 2 } )$ be a random vector with mean vector $ \left( \begin{array} { c } { { 5 } } \\ { { 2 } } \\ { { 0 } } \end{array} \right) $

options.A: nd covariance matrix $ \left( \begin{array} { l l l } { 1 0 } & { 0 . 5 } & { - 0 . 5 } \\ { 0 . 5 } & { 7 } & { 1 . 5 } \\ { - 0 . 5 } & { 1 . 5 } & { 2 } \end{array} \right) $ Then the value of the multiple correlation coefficient between Y and its best linear predictor on $X _ { 1 }$ and $X _ { 2 }$ equals (round off to 2 decimal places).

solution:
The multiple correlation coefficient $R _ { Y | X _ { 1 } , X _ { 2 } }$ is calculated using the covariance matrix and the formula for the coefficient:

 $ R _ { Y | X _ { 1 } , X _ { 2 } } = \surd $ 

Using the covariance matrix and calculating the necessary values gives $R \approx 0 . 1 6$

Thus, the value of the multiple correlation coefficient is 0.16.
Quick Tip

For calculating the multiple correlation coefficient, use the formula involving variances and covariances from the covariance matrix.
